note: Readiness scores are directional. Use this scorecard to surface blockers before launch, not as a substitute for a full security, legal, or compliance review.

launch_types.name: Prototype / demo
launch_types.description: Internal demo or limited alpha. Lower reliability and compliance expectations.

launch_types.name: Closed beta
launch_types.description: Invite-only beta with real user data. Need basic privacy, observability, and incident response.

launch_types.name: Production launch
launch_types.description: Public or paid launch. Requires full security, SLA, compliance, and cost controls.

questions.text: Use case, success criteria, and failure modes are documented.
Human-in-the-loop boundaries and escalation paths are defined.
Rate limits, concurrency caps, and cost kill-switches are configured.
Agent actions can be paused, rolled back, or manually approved.
Multi-region or failover strategy is planned if production.
Primary and fallback LLM providers are chosen.
Model versions are pinned and upgrade policy is documented.
P50/P95 latency targets are set and validated.
Evaluation dataset or benchmark is run before each release.
Prompt injection / jailbreak mitigations are in place.
Data types processed by the agent are inventoried.
Retention and deletion policies are configured.
User consent or lawful basis is documented.
Access / deletion / portability requests can be fulfilled.
Cross-border transfers and DPA/subprocessors are reviewed.
API keys and secrets are not hardcoded; use a secrets manager.
Authentication and authorization are enforced.
User inputs are validated and sanitized.
LLM outputs are filtered before acting on external systems.
Audit logs of agent decisions and tool calls are retained.
Retry, timeout, and circuit-breaker patterns are implemented.
Uptime target is set and provider SLAs are reviewed.
Incident response / escalation runbook exists.
Critical state and vector stores are backed up.
Canary or staged rollout process is in place.
Traces or logs capture request → model → tool flow.
Key metrics (latency, cost, errors, quality) are tracked.
Alerts exist for error spikes, cost spikes, and SLA breaches.
Periodic quality / drift evaluation is scheduled.
Monthly LLM, hosting, and infra budget is set.
Cost per request / per user is estimated.
Spend caps or quotas are configured with alerts.
Pricing model (free, usage-based, seat) is defined.
Launch checklist is signed off by owner.
User communication and rollback plan are ready.
Support / feedback channel is set up.

maturity_levels.name: Experiment
maturity_levels.summary: Not launch-ready. Core gaps in architecture, security, or cost control.

maturity_levels.name: Internal prototype
maturity_levels.summary: Safe for internal demos. Needs hardening before any real users or data.

maturity_levels.name: Closed beta ready
maturity_levels.summary: Ready for limited beta with known guardrails and close monitoring.

maturity_levels.name: Production ready
maturity_levels.summary: Ready for public or paid launch with normal operational oversight.

maturity_levels.name: Enterprise ready
maturity_levels.summary: Strong controls across all dimensions; suitable for high-stakes deployments.

risk_levels.name: Low
risk_levels.summary: Limited blast radius. Standard precautions are sufficient.

risk_levels.name: Moderate
risk_levels.summary: Real user or business impact possible. Address top blockers before launch.

risk_levels.name: High
risk_levels.summary: Significant compliance, financial, or reputational risk. Full review required.

risk_levels.name: Critical
risk_levels.summary: Do not launch without executive, legal, and security sign-off.
